requirements.full_sentence:
2.1 External Commercial Borrowings (ECB): ECBs are commercial loans raised

maximum all-in-cost ceiling, etc.

requirements.full_sentence: Under the approval route, the prospective borrowers are required to send their requests to the RBI through their ADs for examination.

requirements.full_sentence: The call and put option, if any, for FCCBs shall not be exercisable prior to 5 years.

requirements.full_sentence: Entities engaged in micro-finance activities to be eligible to raise ECB: (i) should have a satisfactory borrowing relationship for at least three years with an AD Category I bank in India, and (ii) should have a certificate of due diligence on 'fit and proper' status from the AD Category I bank.

requirements.full_sentence: The certificate of due diligence should comprise the following: (i) that the lender maintains an account with the bank at least for a period of two years, (ii) that the lending entity is organised as per the local laws and held in good esteem by the business/local community, and (iii) that there is no criminal action pending against it.
requirements.object: that the lender maintains an account with the bank at least for a period of two years, (ii) that the lending entity is org

requirements.full_sentence:
2.14 Foreign Currency Exchangeable Bonds (FCEBs): FCEBs can be issued only
under the approval route and shall have minimum maturity of 5 years.
requirements.object: 5 years

requirements.full_sentence:
Issuance of FCEBs shall conform to the
provisions contained in Regulation 21 of the Foreign Exchange Management
(Transfer or Issue of any Foreign Security) Regulations, 2000 read with Schedule IV
to the Regulations which contain eligibilities in respect of the issuer, offered
company, subscriber, permitted end-uses, etc.
requirements.object: contained in Regulation 21 of the Foreign Exchange Management (Transfer or Issue of any Foreign Security) Regulations, 2000

The all-in-cost of FCEBs should be
within the ceiling specified by RBI for ECB.

requirements.full_sentence:
Changes/Modifications in the Drawdown/Repayment Schedule: Designated
AD Category I banks may approve changes / modifications (irrespective of the
number of occasions) in the draw-down and repayment schedules of the ECB
whether associated with change in the average maturity period or not and/ or with
changes (increase/ decrease) in the all-in-cost.
requirements.object: (irrespective of the number of occasions) in the draw-down and repayment schedules of the ECB whether associated with

requirements.full_sentence:
Changes in the Currency of Borrowing: Designated AD Category I banks may
allow changes in the currency of borrowing of the ECB to any other freely convertible
currency or to INR subject to compliance with other prescribed parameters.
requirements.object: currency of borrowing of the ECB to any other freely convertible currency or to INR subject to compliance with other prescribed p

requirements.full_sentence:
Changes in the name of the Borrower Company: Designated AD Category I
banks may allow changes in the name of the borrower company subject to
production of supporting documents evidencing the change in the name from the
Registrar of Companies/ appropriate authority.
requirements.object: name of the borrower company subject to production of supporting documents evidencing the change in the name from the Registrar o

requirements.full_sentence:
Transfer of ECB: Designated AD Category I banks may allow the cases requiring
transfer of the ECB from one company to another on account of re-organisation at
the borrower's level in the form of merger/ demerger/ amalgamation/ acquisition duly
as per the applicable laws/ rules after satisfying themselves that the company
acquiring the ECB is an eligible borrower.
requirements.object: transfer of the ECB from one company to another on account of re-organisation at the borrower's level in the form of merger/

requirements.full_sentence:
Change in the recognized lender: Designated ADs Category I may approve the
requests from the ECB borrowers for change in the recognized lender provided (a)
the original lender as well as the new lender are recognised lender as per extant
ECB guidelines and, (b) there is no change in the other terms and conditions of the
ECB.
requirements.object: the ECB borrowers for change in the recognized lender provided (a) the original lender as well as the new lender are recogni

requirements.full_sentence:
Change in the name of Lender: Designated AD Category I banks may permit
changes in the name of the lender of ECB after satisfying themselves with the
bonafides of the transactions and ensuring that the ECB continues to be in
compliance with applicable guidelines.
requirements.object: name of the lender of ECB after satisfying themselves with the bonafides of the transactions and ensuring that the ECB continues

requirements.full_sentence:
Prepayment of ECB: Prepayment of ECB may be allowed by AD Category I
banks subject to compliance with the stipulated minimum average maturity as
applicable to the contracted loan under these guidelines.
requirements.object: Category I banks subject to compliance with the stipulated minimum average maturity as applicable to the contracted loan under these

requirements.full_sentence:
Cancellation of LRN: The designated AD Category I banks may directly
approach DSIM for cancellation of LRN for ECBs contracted, subject to ensuring that
no draw down against the said LRN has taken place and the monthly ECB-2 returns
till date in respect of the allotted LRN have been submitted to DSIM.
requirements.object: cancellation of LRN for ECBs contracted

requirements.full_sentence:
Change in the end-use of ECB proceeds: The designated AD Category I banks
may approve requests from ECB borrowers for change in end-use in respect of
ECBs availed of under the automatic route, provided the proposed end-use is
permissible under the automatic route as per the extant ECB guidelines7.
requirements.object: borrowers for change in end-use in respect of ECBs availed of under the automatic route

requirements.full_sentence:
Reduction in amount of ECB: Designated AD Category I banks may approve
reduction in the amount of ECB (irrespective of the number of occasions) with or
without any changes in draw-down and repayment schedules, average maturity
period and all-in-cost duly ensuring compliance with the applicable ECB guidelines.
requirements.object: amount of ECB (irrespective of the number of occasions) with or without any changes in draw-down and repayment schedules, ave

requirements.full_sentence:
Change in all-in-cost of ECB: The designated AD Category I banks may
approve requests from ECB borrowers for changes (decrease/increase) in all-in-cost
of the ECBs irrespective of the number of occasions subject to the applicable ECB
norms for automatic route.
requirements.object: borrowers for changes (decrease/increase) in all-in-cost of the ECBs irrespective of the number of occasions subject to the

requirements.full_sentence:
Refinancing of existing ECB: The designated AD Category I bank may allow
refinancing of existing ECB by raising fresh ECB provided the outstanding maturity of
the original borrowing is not reduced and all-in-cost of fresh ECB is lower than the
existing ECB.
requirements.object: ECB by raising fresh ECB provided the outstanding maturity of the original borrowing is not reduced and all-in-cost of f

refinancing of ECBs raised under the previous ECB framework may also be

Extension of matured but unpaid ECB : The designated AD Category I bank

requirements.full_sentence:
2.17 Borrowing by Entities under Investigation: All entities against which
investigation / adjudication / appeal by the law enforcing agencies for violation of any
of the provisions of the Regulations under FEMA pending, may raise ECBs as per
the applicable norms, if they are otherwise eligible, notwithstanding the pending
investigations / adjudications / appeals, without prejudice to the outcome of such
investigations / adjudications / appeals.

pendency of such investigation / adjudication / appeal to the AD Cat-I bank / RBI as

requirements.full_sentence:
In case of borrowing in
INR, the non-resident lender, should mobilise INR through swaps/outright sale
undertaken through an AD Category-I bank in India.
requirements.object: sale undertaken through an AD Category-I bank in India

requirements.full_sentence: 2.23.7 All-in-cost: Shall be mutually agreed between the borrower and the lender.
requirements.object: the borrower and the lender

requirements.full_sentence:
and
shall comply with foreign direct investment / foreign portfolio investment / or any
other norms applicable for foreign lenders / entities holding such securities.
requirements.object: investment / foreign portfolio investment / or any other norms applicable for foreign lenders / entities holding such secur

requirements.full_sentence:
The overall ceiling for such ECBs shall be USD 10
billion or equivalent.
requirements.object: or equivalent

requirements.full_sentence:
However, OMCs should have a Board approved forex mark to
market procedure and prudent risk management policy, for such ECBs.
requirements.object: forex mark to market procedure and prudent risk management policy, for such ECBs

requirements.full_sentence:
3.2 Available route43 of borrowing: 44Any proposal of borrowing by eligible Indian
entities for issuance of these bonds will be examined at Foreign Exchange
Department, Central Office, Mumbai and such request should be forwarded through
AD bank only.45 46 47
requirements.object: bank only

requirements.full_sentence:
3.3.1 Minimum Maturity: 48Minimum original maturity period for Rupee denominated
bonds raised up to USD 50 million equivalent in INR per financial year should be 3
years and for bonds raised above USD 50 million equivalent in INR per financial year
should be 5 years.49.
requirements.object: for bonds raised above USD 50 million equivalent in INR per financial year should be 5 years

requirements.full_sentence:
The call and put option, if any, shall not be exercisable prior to
completion of minimum maturity.
requirements.object: completion of minimum maturity

requirements.full_sentence: 47 dated June 07, 2017 Deleted portion read as "The bonds shall have minimum maturity of three years"
requirements.object: three years"

requirements.full_sentence: should not be a country identified in the public statement of the FATF as:
requirements.object: in the public statement of the FATF as:

requirements.full_sentence: Activities prohibited as per the foreign direct investment guidelines;
requirements.object: direct investment guidelines

requirements.full_sentence:
3.3.6 Exchange Rate for conversion: The exchange rate for foreign currency -
Rupee conversion shall be the market rate on the date of settlement for the purpose
of transactions undertaken for issue and servicing of the bonds
requirements.object: on the date of settlement for the purpose of transactions undertaken for issue and servicing of the bonds

requirements.full_sentence:
3.3.8 Leverage Ratio: The borrowing by financial institutions under the Framework
shall be subject to the leverage ratio prescribed, if any, by the sectoral regulator as
per the prudential norms.
requirements.object: leverage ratio prescribed

requirements.full_sentence:
61 62
54 Deleted the words "shall not have access to these bonds but" vide A.P.(DIR Series) Circular No 14 dated November 3, 2016 55 Inserted vide A.
requirements.object: bonds but" vide A

requirements.full_sentence: 47 dated June 07, 2017 Deleted portion read as "The all-in-cost of borrowing by issuance of Rupee denominated bonds should be commensurate with prevailing market conditions".
requirements.object: market conditions"

requirements.full_sentence:
66Borrowers issuing Rupee denominated bonds
overseas should incorporate clause in the agreement / offer document so as to
enable them to obtain the list of primary bond holders and provide the same to the
regulatory authorities in India as and when required.
requirements.object: agreement / offer document so as to enable them to obtain the list of primary bond holders and provide the same to the regul

requirements.full_sentence:
The agreement / offer document
should also state that the bonds can only be sold / transferred / offered as security
overseas subject to compliance with aforesaid IOSCO / FATF jurisdictional
requirements.
requirements.object: bonds can only be sold / transferred / offered as security overseas subject to compliance with aforesaid IOSCO / FATF jurisdiction

requirements.full_sentence:
Routing of funds raised abroad to India: It may be noted that:
i.

Indian companies or their ADs using or establishing structures which contravene
the above shall render themselves liable for penal action as prescribed under
FEMA.
requirements.object: penal action as prescribed under FEMA

requirements.full_sentence: 6 dated September 22, 2017 Deleted portion read as "AD Category -I banks should report to the Foreign Exchange Department, External Commercial Borrowings Division, Central Office, Shahid Bhagat Singh Road, Fort, Mumbai 400001 the figures of actual drawdown (s)/ repayment (s) by their constituent borrowers quoting the loan registration number.
requirements.object: Exchange Department, External Commercial Borrowings Division, Central Office, Shahid Bhagat Singh Road, Fort, Mumbai 400001 the

requirements.full_sentence: Such reporting by e-mail to shall be made on the date of transaction itself.
requirements.object: date of transaction itself

requirements.full_sentence:
Imports should be as permissible under the extant Foreign Trade Policy of
the Director General of Foreign Trade (DGFT).
requirements.object: the extant Foreign Trade Policy of the Director General of Foreign Trade (DGFT)

requirements.full_sentence:
For
trade credit up to five years, the ab-initio contract period should be 6 (six) months.

The period is reckoned from the date of shipment and the
guarantee period should be co-terminus with the period of credit.
requirements.object: period of credit

requirements.full_sentence:
5.6.1 Monthly reporting: AD Category I banks are required to furnish details of
approvals, drawal, utilisation, and repayment of Trade Credit approved by all its
branches, in a consolidated statement, during a month, in form TC to the Director,
Division of International Trade and Finance, Department of Economic Policy and
Research, RBI, Central Office, Fort, Mumbai - 400 001 (and in MS-Excel file through
email) so as to reach not later than 10th of the following month.
requirements.object: approvals, drawal, utilisation, and repayment of Trade Credit approved by all its branches, in a consolidated statement, during

requirements.full_sentence:
Each trade credit
may be given a unique identification number by the AD bank.
requirements.object: identification number by the AD bank

requirements.full_sentence:
6.1 Borrowing in foreign currency by an Authorised Dealer: An authorised
dealer in India may borrow in foreign currency in the circumstances and subject to
the conditions mentioned below:
i.
requirements.object: in the circumstances and subject to the conditions mentioned below: i

requirements.full_sentence:
The borrowing may be from the Head Office or branch or correspondent
outside India of the authorised dealer or any other entity as permitted by RBI.
requirements.object: Office or branch or correspondent outside India of the authorised dealer or any other entity as permitted by RBI

requirements.full_sentence:
The aggregate amount of borrowing by all branches of authorised dealer from
all permitted sources shall be upto hundred percent of the unimpaired Tier I
capital of the authorised dealer or such other limit as decided by the RBI from
time to time, or US$ 10 million, whichever is more.
requirements.object: of the unimpaired Tier I capital of the authorised dealer or such other limit as decided by the RBI from time to time, or US$

requirements.full_sentence:
A branch outside India of an authorised dealer may borrow in the normal
course of its banking business outside India, subject to the directions or
guidelines issued RBI from time to time, and the Regulatory Authority of the
country where the branch is located.
requirements.object: course of its banking business outside India

requirements.full_sentence:
An authorised dealer may borrow from a bank or a financial institution outside
India, for the purpose of granting pre-shipment or post-shipment credit to his
exporter constituent in India
v.
requirements.object: or a financial institution outside India, for the purpose of granting pre-shipment or post-shipment credit to his exporter constitu

requirements.full_sentence:
The borrowing shall be subject to compliance with prudential norms, interest
rate directives and guidelines, if any, issued by RBI from time to time
requirements.object: with prudential norms, interest rate directives and guidelines

requirements.full_sentence:
6.2 Lending in foreign currency by an Authorised Dealer: An authorised dealer in
India or his branch outside India may lend in foreign currency in the circumstances
and subject to the conditions mentioned below:
i.
requirements.object: in the circumstances and subject to the conditions mentioned below: i

requirements.full_sentence:
The loans may be extended by a branch outside India of an authorised dealer
in the normal course of its banking business outside India;
requirements.object: branch outside India of an authorised dealer in the normal course of its banking business outside India

requirements.full_sentence:
The loans may be extended by an authorised dealer to its constituents in India
for meeting their foreign exchange requirements or for their rupee working
capital requirements or capital expenditure
iii.
requirements.object: authorised dealer to its constituents in India for meeting their foreign exchange requirements or for their rupee working capital re

requirements.full_sentence:
Credit facilities may be extended to a wholly owned subsidiary abroad or a
joint venture abroad of an Indian entity;
requirements.object: wholly owned subsidiary abroad or a joint venture abroad of an Indian entity

requirements.full_sentence:
provided that not less than 51 per cent
of equity in such subsidiary or joint venture is held by the Indian entity and
that the credit facilities are in compliance with the Foreign Exchange
Management (Transfer and Issue of Foreign Security) Regulations, 2000;
requirements.object: cent of equity in such subsidiary or joint venture is held by the Indian entity and that the credit facilities are in compliance with

requirements.full_sentence:
Loan may be granted to the constituents maintaining RFC Account, against
the security of funds held in such account;
requirements.object: constituents maintaining RFC Account, against the security of funds held in such account

requirements.full_sentence:
Loans may be extended by an authorised dealer or its branch outside India
against the security of funds held in NRE/FCNR (B) deposit accounts in
accordance with the Foreign Exchange Management (Deposit) Regulations,
2000;
requirements.object: authorised dealer or its branch outside India against the security of funds held in NRE/FCNR (B) deposit accounts in accordance with

requirements.full_sentence: Loans may be extended by an authorised dealer to another authorised dealer.
requirements.object: authorised dealer to another authorised dealer

requirements.full_sentence:
For execution of projects outside India and for exports on deferred
payment terms: A person resident in India may borrow, whether by way of
loan or overdraft or any other credit facility, from a bank situated outside India,
for execution outside India of a turnkey project or civil construction contract or
in connection with exports on deferred payment terms, provided the terms and
conditions stipulated by the authority which has granted the approval to the
project or contract or export is in accordance with the Foreign Exchange
Management (Export of Goods and Services) Regulations, 2000.
requirements.object: of loan or overdraft or any other credit facility, from a bank situated outside India, for execution outside India of a turnkey project or civil construction contract or in connection with exports on deferred payment terms

requirements.full_sentence:
For imports: An importer in India may, for import of goods into India, avail of
foreign currency credit for a period not exceeding six months extended by the
overseas supplier of goods, provided the import is in compliance with the
Export Import Policy of the Government of India in force.
requirements.object: goods into India, avail of foreign currency credit for a period not exceeding six months extended by the overseas supplier of goods

requirements.full_sentence:
Borrowing by resident individual: An individual resident in India may
borrow a sum not exceeding US$ 250,000/- or its equivalent from his close
relative outside India, subject to the conditions that:
a.
requirements.object: exceeding US$ 250,000/- or its equivalent from his close relative outside India

requirements.full_sentence:
Lending to WOS / JV: An Indian entity may lend to its wholly owned
subsidiary or joint venture abroad constituted in accordance with the
provisions of Foreign Exchange Management (Transfer or issue of foreign
security) Regulations, 2000.
requirements.object: owned subsidiary or joint venture abroad constituted in accordance with the provisions of Foreign Exchange Management (Transfer or

requirements.full_sentence:
Lending by Select Institutions: Export Import Bank of India, Industrial
Development Bank of India, Industrial Finance Corporation of India, Industrial
Credit and Investment Corporation of India Limited, Small Industries
Development Bank of India Limited or any other institution in India may extend
loans to their constituents in India out of the foreign currency borrowings
raised by these institutions with the approval of the Central Government for
the purpose of onward lending.
requirements.object: constituents in India out of the foreign currency borrowings raised by these institutions with the approval of the Central Gover

requirements.full_sentence:
Lending by Indian companies to their employees: Indian companies in
India may grant loans to the employees of their branches outside India for
personal purposes provided that the loan shall be granted for personal
purposes in accordance with the lender's Staff Welfare Scheme/Loan Rules
and other terms and conditions as applicable to its staff resident in India and
abroad.
requirements.object: purposes in accordance with the lender's Staff Welfare Scheme/Loan Rules and other terms and conditions as applicable to its s

requirements.full_sentence:
In cases
where a Rupee facility which is either fund based or non-fund based (such as letter
of credit / guarantee / letter of undertaking / letter of comfort) or is in the form of
derivative contract by residents that are subsidiaries of multinational companies, is
guaranteed by a non-resident (non resident group entity in case of derivative
contracts), there is no transaction involving foreign exchange until the guarantee is
invoked and the non-resident guarantor is required to meet the liability under the
guarantee.
requirements.object: under the guarantee

requirements.full_sentence:
The arrangements shall be with the following terms:
i.
requirements.object: terms: i

requirements.full_sentence:
The non-resident guarantor may discharge the liability by i) payment out of
rupee balances held in India or ii) by remitting the funds to India or iii) by debit
to his FCNR(B)/NRE account maintained with an AD bank in India.
requirements.object: i) payment out of rupee balances held in India or ii) by remitting the funds to India or iii) by debit to his FCNR(B)/NRE a

requirements.full_sentence:
In such cases, the non-resident guarantor may enforce his claim against the
resident borrower to recover the amount and on recovery he may seek
repatriation of the amount if the liability is discharged either by inward
remittance or by debit to FCNR(B)/NRE account.
requirements.object: the resident borrower to recover the amount and on recovery he may seek repatriation of the amount if the liability is disch

conditions:
where the liability is met by the non-resident out of funds remitted to
India or by debit to his FCNR(B)/ NRE account
requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where the liability is met by the non-resident out of funds remitted to
India or by debit to his FCNR(B)/ NRE account, the repayment may be made
by credit to the FCNR(B)/ NRE/ NRO account of the guarantor provided, the
amount remitted/credited shall not exceed the rupee equivalent of the amount
paid by the non-resident guarantor against the invoked guarantee.
requirements.object: of the amount paid by the non-resident guarantor against the invoked guarantee

requirements.full_sentence:
AD Category I banks are required to furnish at quarterly interval details of
guarantees availed of/ invoked, by all its branches, in a format specified by
RBI, to the Principal Chief General Manager, Foreign Exchange Department,
ECB Division, Reserve Bank of India, Central Office Building, 11th floor, Fort,
Mumbai - 400 001 so as to reach the Department not later than 10th day of
the month following quarter to which the data pertain to.
requirements.object: interval details of guarantees availed of/ invoked, by all its branches, in a format specified by RBI, to the Principal Chief

requirements.full_sentence:
The underlying debt instrument should have a minimum average maturity of
three years;
requirements.object: maturity of three years

conditions:
in case the novated
loan is designated in foreign currency
requirements.full_sentence:
Non-Banking Financial Companies - Infrastructure Finance Companies
proposing to avail of the credit enhancement facility should comply with the
eligibility criteria and prudential norms laid down in the circular DNBS.PD.CC
No.168/03.02.089/2009-10 dated February 12, 2010 and in case the novated
loan is designated in foreign currency, the IFC should hedge the entire foreign
currency exposure;
requirements.object: criteria and prudential norms laid down in the circular DNBS
